summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that an unauthenticated attacker can write arbitrary PHP code to payment handler files via the /payment/api/editable/update endpoint. The written code is later executed when the payment service processes a request via require statements in PaymentService::payListener.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should allow writing arbitrary PHP content to server-side payment handler files (e.g., before_pay.php) without authentication. The overwritten file should contain attacker-controlled content that executes when the payment processing pipeline invokes require on the file.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built a Laravel application with the pay-uz package, sent an unauthenticated GET request to /payment/api/editable/update with file_name=before_pay and arbitrary PHP content, and confirmed that the file was overwritten with attacker-controlled code containing the proof string.
verification_method: The exploit script writes a unique proof string (EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF_<timestamp>) to a payment handler PHP file, then verifies the file content contains the proof string. If path traversal is tested, it checks that public/index.php was overwritten. The verification script confirms the proof string exists in either the payment handler file or the overwritten index.php.
evidence_produced_by_run: The file at app/Http/Controllers/Payments/before_pay.php was overwritten with attacker-controlled PHP code containing the proof string.
The HTTP response from /payment/api/editable/update confirmed successful file write (success response).
The unauthenticated GET request bypassed CSRF protection because Route::any() accepts GET requests which are excluded from Laravel's VerifyCsrfToken middleware.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub on the host, checks out the master branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
